programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Perth & Kinross for a couple who have a £450,000 budget. Along the way, she gets up close and personal with some of the region's tallest trees(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Keely and Bruce in Hucknall, Nottingham, to create a garden that successfully integrates a hot tub into the finished design(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of the south east of England. Along the way, they encounter items including a potter's wheel, before an auction in Tring, Hertfordshire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out South
programme.desc: Ben Moore meets the villagers who feel under attack by noisy motorbikes day and night - and the bikers who say they're proud to be loud as it makes them safer on our roads. As a national shortage of professional chefs threatens our curry houses, food entrepreneur Sarah Ali Choudhury, who grew up in her family's restaurant in Dorset, is calling for more women to get involved(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked by the hostility of the other mums at Ollie's new school, and shares her concerns with Mick. Louise hatches a plan to help Bex take her mind off university. Kathy learns Bobby is staying with Max and pleads with her grandson to reconsider. Phil and Sharon realise Mel has been taking money from the E20 account and cut her off - leaving her unable to pay for Hunter's funeral(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: TV and radio presenter Rickie Haywood-Williams, legendary Motown singer Martha Reeves, Olympic gold medal-winning long jumper Greg Rutherford, actress Elizabeth Bourgine and heavyweight boxer Dillian Whyte take part, as the competition hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace continues. The first challenge for the five hopefuls is an Invention Test - the infamous test of imagination and creativity. The contestants are all given a mincer and a burger press and are tasked with making just one dish - a burger and fries - of their own design. Ingredients to choose from include tuna, mackerel, beef chuck, pork belly, lamb shoulder and turkey breast(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: It's the most important day of the year for Kerry - the annual steam fair. This year it falls on her birthday and Kurtan has organised her a surprise present. However, the gift involves some walking, and so the cousins embark on a cross-country trek. This affords them the time to share some local village myths with the crew, such as the legend of the 'Fox Twins'(n)

programme.title: Murder in Successville
programme.desc: Sleet and sidekick Emma Bunton go to North Successville, where the detective investigates the murder of a fellow cop and takes a liking to the area's chief of police. Three likely suspects soon emerge - the owner of a diner, a local fisherman and a disgraced former police officer. An approaching storm forces Sleet and Emma to seek shelter in a forest - which leads to a showdown with the killer(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sarah Millican hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, west London, treating the audience to her own brand of humour, before introducing routines by camp comic Joe Lycett and award-winning funnyman Russell Kane(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present the show live from Staffordshire Police's dog training centre, where they meet a dog who was injured in the line of duty(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress on a Cabus bungalow, a three-storey house in Newport, and two lots by the side of a canal and river in West Stockwith(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A residential street is evacuated as a result of a major gas escape which sees one of the workers trapped underground, and fly-tippers come under investigation(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles examines the rescue of a man missing overnight after being swept out to sea on his surf board. A resident comes to the aid of a woman who fell into a city-centre river(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of married couples look for items at the Royal Highland Centre in Ingliston, Edinburgh, with help from experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper. Thomas Plant presents(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al discovers that his mother has been keeping a secret from him, Josh's parents face a heartbreaking decision, and Sid helps a new father to cope with his noisy neighbour(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a Suffolk house hunter find a low-maintenance rural home with a wildlife-friendly garden for £490,000. Plus, a look at traditional boatbuilding skills(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a Swansea couple who want to redesign their garden to be safe for their children and allow them to grow vegetables(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of Northamptonshire and Bedfordshire. Items up for sale include a ring made of lava from Mount Vesuvius and depleted uranium glassware(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby wakes up in a park and heads back to the Square, where his behaviour concerns Callum - but when he tries to help the troubled teen runs off and bumps into Rainie. Kathy and Ian blame each other for Bobby struggling to settle back into Walford, and Mel speaks to Jay about scaling back Hunter's funeral - but she is stunned with what he tells her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric fears for his health, leading him to withdraw just as his granddaughter goes into premature labour. Cameron steps in to take charge, and forces Ric to face up to his fears. Essie's false claim to be Isla's adoptive mother stands to be exposed when the girl requires emergency surgery, and Chloe throws herself back into work in the hope of moving on from Evan - but is gripped by a paranoid belief that someone is spying on her(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Shaun is interrogated by the CIA in a covert safe house, and while Rachel sees CCTV footage of him going in, she then finds she is unable to locate him inside. Her investigation leads her to make shocking discoveries about the degree to which CCTV footage might be manipulated. Finally released by his American captors, Shaun goes on the run from the police - but his quest to clear his name soon takes a devastating turn(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Detective Hoggy is on the hunt for a heroin addict and prolific thief who is on the run for multiple offences. When they finally catch him, he makes it clear that he has nothing left to lose, and is ready to give the team a tough time when they take him in for questioning. Elsewhere, a 27-year-old addict has been arrested 69 times as she continues offending to fund her habit(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Live from Birmingham, Michelle Ackerley discovers the history that inspired BBC television series Peaky Blinders. In the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest appeals and CCTV(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts revisits an old church in Denby, Derbyshire, which was being turned into a home in 2007, and there are also visits to Milton Regis in Kent and Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate a factory fire that forces residents to evacuate their homes, and a tipper truck is caught on camera as it illegally dumps 20 tons of waste(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A professional British cyclist is involved in a horrific collision with a race support vehicle, and two men using an accelerant to start a bonfire get more than they bargained for(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Leominster in Herefordshire, where experts Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant advise two teams competing to win the greater profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma and Jimmi are there for baby Josh when he needs them most, while Al gets more than he bargained for when Eve confronts him about his underhand tactics(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple looking for a home in rural Cambridgeshire, and also visits National Trust nature reserve Wicken Fen - one of the most important wetlands in Europe.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Northampton that will allow two children with limited mobility access to the outdoor space(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton head to Lincolnshire, Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire. James tries out a local delicacy and Izzie tries her hand at a winter sport(n)

programme.desc: By the Liberal Democrats(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at the Elizabeth Garrett Anderson school in North London(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at life in the In The Style offices in Greater Manchester and the new world of ultra-fast fashion. The series follows the work of 32-year-old business owner Adam Frisby and his team during the summer season, as the company competes with rival fast fashion brands to sign up the latest talent, come up with the hottest styles and supply them faster and better than any other brand. In the first episode, social media influencer Lorna Luxe launches her new summer range and has big plans for a glamorous shoot(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines the ways supermarkets fight for customers' loyalty, and visits a farm claiming to be using technology to produce healthy chicken at a low cost. Elsewhere, Gregg meets Asda's head of fruit and vegetables to find out why it is a challenge to remove plastic packaging from products, and Nikki Fox sees how they are using a virtual supermarket to see what appeals to us most(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold Hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A fishing boat in Salcombe goes up in flames with the skipper still aboard, while on the Isle of Sheppey, the lifeboat crew race to the aid of a man stuck in freezing mud in the middle of the night. A dog falls into the river Thames, creating an emergency for the Chiswick crew when the owner dives in to save it - and soon needs rescuing herself(n)

programme.title: This Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: House-proud couples from the seaside towns of Weston-super-Mare and Bournemouth swap homes to try out a new way of life, and see whether they enjoy the experience(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A search for relatives helps to bring back together a family who have spent a lifetime apart. Plus, the story of a man who helped changed public perception of disability guide dogs(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the UK, starting with the west of Britain. Fiinds include the lost First World War training trenches on Salisbury Plain, and Britain's first double henge - discovered just down the road from Stonehenge(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Bafta-winning actress Julie Walters, of Educating Rita, Mamma Mia! and Harry Potter fame, traces her roots back to Co Mayo in western Ireland, where her great-grandfather Anthony Clark was one of the first members of the Land League. The organisation was formed in 1879 and campaigned for more than 40 years for the rights of tenant farmers to own their own land(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Nick Davies and David Harper are on hand in Reading, Berkshire to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Presented by Paul Martin(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Barbecue chefs Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn travel around Wales to create huge outdoor feasts to reward the work of a variety of organisations and individuals. They begin by celebrating football club Barry Town United's promotion to the Welsh Premier League, including cooking a whole pig, Hawaiian-style, in a huge brick fire pit, to be served with pit beans, cornbread and an apple and fennel slaw, with fruit-filled toffee apples for dessert(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea uses his expertise to make a dilapidated and unsafe go-kart roadworthy once again. Kirsten Ramsay faces an unusual challenge in the shape of a papier mache cat, while saddlemaker Suzie Fletcher is called upon to restore a hand-crafted leather gun slip, commissioned 30 years ago by a man as a gift for his father's 60th birthday(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as cricket enthusiasts the Wickets take on singers the Choristers. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Alice Band, Whistle Whistle, Hall Book and Blass Wine(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The ninth first-round match sees four students from LSE pit their wits against a team from the Courtauld Institute of Art in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Party's militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Melinda Messenger presents highlights of 1997, the year the Teletubbies hit the small screen in a blaze of colour, Bridget Jones's Diary was every woman's best friend, and Viagra gave hope to flagging men worldwide. Movies that stole the show were blockbuster Titanic and The Full Monty, which boosted both the British film industry and Sheffield's tourism(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: China: A New World Order
programme.desc: The concluding film explores the growing tension between China and the West over allegations that the Chinese state has engaged for years in large-scale industrial espionage and hacking to steal the West's business secrets and technological know-how. China denies any such wrongdoing - but growing anger in the US is fuelling the trade war with President Xi Jinping's superpower(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: Bob is offered advice by his friend Paddy Chayefsky about his autobiographical film All That Jazz, and hears Gwen's account of their relationship with him and the making of Chicago. Years later Bob and Gwen reminisce in a bar and Bob tells her he has been offered to direct the revival of Sweet Charity and declined, but suggests that Gwen take the role of supervisor to recreate the original dance numbers. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star in the final episode of the biographical drama about the lives of choreographer-director Bob Fosse and celebrated Broadway dancer Gwen Verdon(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The contestants are challenged to design a small studio in a student halls of residence in Leicester, which must allow room for sleeping, studying and socialising. With just two days to complete their makeovers, the competitors need to make the best use of their time to ensure they get their studios finished. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Northumberland, where Helen Skelton visits a scheme in Blyth in which students from disadvantaged areas work with retired engineers and craftsmen to learn traditional boat-building and shipwright skills. Adam Henson meets the next contestant hoping to lift the trophy in this year's One Man and His Dog competition and Matt Baker has all the information on how viewers can take part in this year's Countryfile Ramble for Children in Need(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Keely and Bruce in Hucknall, Nottingham, to create a garden that successfully integrates a hot tub into the finished design(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers crack down on shops illegally selling knives to under-18s. Plus, former EastEnders actress Brooke Kinsella talks about her brother Ben who was stabbed to death(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fly to Enniskillen, Co Fermanagh, to fish for pike, which eluded them in the previous series. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample traditional refreshments. Paul demonstrates how to perform life-saving CPR and Bob gives him a glimpse into his future by telling his fortune in an ancient Irish manner(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives try to find the family of a reclusive printer, and in North London a team unlock the story of a sailor who stopped at nothing to fight for his country(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the final day of the Liberal Democrat party conference in Bournemouth(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane visits the Devon and Cornwall coastlines, joining a fishing expedition on board one of the last remaining Brixham trawlers, which were constructed more than 100 years ago. He explores a string of forts built by Henry VIII, before taking a ferry to the Isles of Scilly. Dick Strawbridge learns about the steam-power revolution pioneered in the tin mines of Cornwall, and Alice Roberts discovers how weather far out at sea generates waves that hit the UK's shoreline(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: South Yorkshire-born actor and adventurer Brian Blessed explores the lives of his ancestors on a journey that takes him to Doncaster, London and Portsmouth. Seeking to discover the source of his spirit for adventure - he is the oldest man to have reached the North Magnetic Pole - the veteran performer uncovers the life of his great-great-grandfather Jabez Blessed, who as an orphan lived rough on the streets of London before growing up to father 13 children(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson appraise a chair made from the wood of Lord Nelson's ship while visiting the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth, Cornwall(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn celebrate the achievements of Clwyd Special Riding School in North Wales with a big cook-out. With more than 100 people to feed, Sam and Shauna organise a menu of 10 huge beef briskets, barbecued in giant cinder-block smoker, built with the help of the community(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a set of bagpipes that saw action in the First World War, a silver Welsh crown of cultural significance, an antique chair, and a quirky tortoise-shaped bell(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A lifeboat crew in Dorset faces the challenge of getting urgent medical aid to a rock climber, who has fallen more than 40 feet and landed on an inaccessible rocky ledge. In Cornwall, a paraglider misjudges his launch and ends up crashing into the sea, while the Redcar crew search for a man who has drifted over a mile offshore in a small toy dinghy(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox helps more people living and working in remote rural areas to find love. The first episode introduces Grace, a sheep farmer from Wales, and Martin, a dairy farmer from Yorkshire. Grace faces inheriting her father's business when he retires, so is looking for a partner who will be willing to share the responsibility. Martin has not had a relationship in more than a decade, so leaves behind his Doncaster roots to attend a speed dating event in London attended by women from across the UK who have written to the show(n)

programme.title: Defending the Guilty
programme.desc: Comedy following idealistic pupil barrister Will Packham, as he is shown the ropes by his cynical, worldly-wise pupilmaster Caroline. Mistakenly believing that his role is to serve justice, Will must navigate his way through a criminal justice system seemingly designed to be as opaque and confusing as possible. Starring Will Sharpe and Katherine Parkinson(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the second round of fixtures, including New York Jets v Cleveland Browns, Green Bay Packers v Minnesota Vikings, and Los Angeles Rams v New Orleans Saints(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life
programme.desc: Stroke victim Peter is determined to start driving again and 19-year-old Antonia undergoes surgery, but hopes to recover in time for her ParalympicsGB trials(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Charity Cheats
programme.desc: Kevin Duala returns to expose more scams denying good causes donations from the public, with more than £2billion falling into the hands of cheats and criminals. The first edition features the stories of a hospice manager who stole £90,000, and a charity boss who syphoned off hundreds of thousands to live the high life in the Caribbean(n)

programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of Northamptonshire and Bedfordshire. Items up for sale include a ring made of lava from Mount Vesuvius and depleted uranium glassware(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a Swansea couple who want to redesign their garden to be safe for their children and allow them to grow vegetables(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the life of the hippopotamus as never seen before. The programme gets closer than ever to an animal that cannot swim yet is utterly dependent on water. In Botswana's Okavango delta, hippos face an unparalleled challenge - deep floodwaters dry to dust in a matter of months. In one extraordinary season, cameras go beneath the surface to see them protect their families and face enemies as they deal with the drought. Going far beyond their dangerous reputation, the programme discovers the true nature of the hippo, an animal that is compassionate, sensitive and highly intelligent(n)

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: A couple from Nottingham and a mother and daughter from Wiltshire sample new lifestyles as they swap homes. After the trial, they reveal what they have learned from the experience(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Return of the programme that follows the work of probate detectives. A man travels 9,000 miles to meet his half-sister for the first time, while a surprise adoption and travelling ancestors threaten to derail the search for a Leicester City fan's heirs(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs in the north of Britain, where finds include evidence for the first Roman siege in the UK. There's also a look at Lindisfarne, which was violently sacked by the Vikings 1,000 years ago(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Tamzin Outhwaite sets out to learn about her Italian roots on the maternal side of her family as a result of her fond childhood memories of big meals with relatives, antipasto and energetic conversations. Her journey begins in the heart of London's East End, where her great-grandfather Tony Gonella once ran a cafe. She visits the Isle of Man, Italy and finally Glasgow as she explores her ancestor's life, and also discovers more about her mother's father Remo Santi and his dad Adelmo(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Will Axon and Christina Trevanion assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, including some intriguing decorative pictures, while Paul Martin looks at the man who saved the hall(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn celebrate Cardiff's Gujarati community with a vegetarian cook-out, building a special giant multi-layered grill that several people can use at once to cook all the vegetables. The duo learn how to make an avocado and mango chutney using a recipe that has been handed down through the generations(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A 100-year-old Nordic rocking chair that its owner's grandparents were given on their wedding day is restored. A mother of pearl shell and a ceramic bulldog are also redeemed(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The four remaining interior designers take part in a commercial design challenge to transform two restaurants in Bristol, as they look to claim their place in the semi-finals. For the first time, Michelle Ogundehin tells them they will have the chance to meet their clients and see the spaces before taking on the design, but also warns them that she needs to see some individual design flair from each of them, as at the end of the challenge, one designer from the losing team will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: The young volunteers begin their third week at the Royal Derby Hospital and Senior Nurse Karen gives them more responsibilities, as she sends four volunteers to two new wards. One of the youngest volunteers on the programme is Fin, who, at 19 years old, wasn't sensitive enough to the needs on the Cancer Ward and was asked to leave. Fin and Michael are moved to the Elderly Care Ward, where they are tasked with providing care for 101-year-old patient John, a wartime Spitfire pilot(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: In the final episode, Max looks forward to her graduation party and despite things not going to plan, has a wonderful unique present from Sam and the girls. Comedy-drama, created by and starring Pamela Adlon(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the case against Alfred Moore, who was sentenced to death for murdering two policemen in Huddersfield in 1951(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Great Irish Journeys with Martha Kearney
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues to trace the footsteps of 19th-century artist and geologist George Victor Du Noyer, examining places of power as seen through his eyes. She traces the movement of power from Newgrange, Co Meath, through the Rock of Cashel, sees Waterford Charter Roll and ends up at Castletown House in Co Kildare(n)

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore charts Rome's rise from the abandonment and neglect of the 14th century into the everlasting seat of the Papacy recognised today. His story begins with the debauchery and decadence of the Renaissance, and takes viewers through the Catholic Reformation and on to the arrival of fascism and the creation of the Vatican State. He steps inside sensational palaces and churches, and talks about some of the world's most beloved art as he brings his exploration of Rome to a close(n)

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel leads him on railway journey through the modern south Indian states of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. He travels from the former princely state of Mysuru, aka Mysore, to the first stronghold of the East India Company in Chennai, formerly Madras(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: David Olusoga examines 50 years of BBC documentary archives to try and discover why dictators can have such a powerful appeal. He uncovers the surprising optimism felt by the West towards men like Gaddafi and Mugabe early in their regimes, and examines events that turned this optimism into horror(n)

programme.title: Summertime (2015)
programme.desc: An ambitious farmer's daughter travels to Paris in 1971, where she falls in love with a radical feminist. She finds herself obliged to return to the family farm, and her new lover accompanies her on the journey, but their relationship is greeted with disapproval. Romantic drama, starring Cecile De France and Izia Higelin. In French(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes' series exploring the lives and teachings of influential ancient philosophers draws to a close with a profile of Confucius, who is credited as the first Chinese thinker to take a systematic philosophical approach to issues of social, political and ethical importance. Born during the chaotic Spring and Autumn period of Chinese history, Confucius believed that harmony could be promoted and achieved through the examples of sage rulers. He was also a innovative teacher, and tried to instill his principles of moral excellence in China's elite, but died without seeing his vision for his nation come to pass. However, his philosophies would eventually form the bedrock of Chinese culture, with many of his principles of tradition surviving to this day(n)

programme.title: Metalworks!
programme.desc: An exploration of how blacksmiths throughout history combined creativity and engineering to make items that served society's needs and changed how the world looked. The programme examines both artistic and mass-produced pieces, and considers the work of designers and smiths including Robert Adam, Jean Tijou, George Gilbert Scott, Robert Bakewell and John Tresilian(n)

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore charts Rome's rise from the abandonment and neglect of the 14th century into the everlasting seat of the Papacy recognised today. His story begins with the debauchery and decadence of the Renaissance, and takes viewers through the Catholic Reformation and on to the arrival of fascism and the creation of the Vatican State. He steps inside sensational palaces and churches, and talks about some of the world's most beloved art as he brings his exploration of Rome to a close(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Great Irish Journeys with Martha Kearney
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes her journey retracing the steps of 19th-century geologist George Victor Du Noyer. Martha explores places of great spirituality, starting with the Blasket Islands off the coast of Co Kerry, then on to the Dingle Peninsula, where Du Noyer discovered stone beehive huts called clochans. Finally, she heads north to the islands of the Fermanagh Lakelands - White Island, Boa Island and Station Island(n)

programme.title: England's Forgotten Queen: The Life and Death of Lady Jane Grey
programme.desc: Historian Helen Castor presents a three-part docu-drama piecing together the story of the woman who was the de facto Queen of England and Ireland for nine days in 1553. In the first episode, Helen investigates an incendiary document, written on his deathbed by King Edward VI, which cuts his sister Mary Tudor out of the line of succession and leaves the throne to his cousin Jane(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre investigates why the IRA believed that 1972 would be the year to bring them victory - only to instead face a massive British military response, as the conflict entered its most violent period. The programme also examines recently discovered evidence of secret talks between the paramilitary movement and the British government - and a possible missed opportunity for a lasting peace(n)

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose discovers the truth behind the first circumnavigation of the world. More than 500 years ago, Ferdinand Magellan set out to find the riches of the Spice Islands, but contrary to popular perception, he failed to reach them. A dramatic sequence of events followed, involving mutiny and murder, as his scurvy-ridden crew continued the journey without him(n)

programme.title: The Last Explorers
programme.desc: Neil Oliver examines the feats of four Scottish adventurers who helped to shape the modern world with their discoveries and ideas, beginning with David Livingstone. The presenter travels down the Zambezi river to reveal how the explorer exploited his celebrity in a bid to end the slave trade and bring commerce, Christianity and civilisation to the African continent(n)

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Scott traces the history of Sicily, from the first arrival of ancient Greek settlers 3,000 years ago to the arrival of the Byzantine Empire. He begins his journey on the slopes of Mount Etna, Europe's largest active volcano, and discovers how the Greeks began to settle on Sicily's east coast - planting their olives and vines and building great city states that soon came to rival even Athens itself(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: In the wake of the First World War, former sapper Thomas Shelby and his brothers return from the trenches to their hometown of Birmingham, where their family runs a feared crime ring - making money from illegal betting, protection and the black market. Thomas recognises an opportunity to move up in the world when a crate of guns goes missing from an arms factory, but government minister Winston Churchill sends in a ruthless police chief from Belfast to impose order on the increasingly lawless city and recover the weapons. First episode of the drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Chief Inspector Campbell carries out a violent raid on the gang's territory and demands a meeting with Thomas, who is later sent a bullet with his name on it after picking a fight with a gypsy family at a country fair. Meanwhile, Aunt Polly is shocked to discover Ada is pregnant, and when Thomas discovers his former war comrade Freddie Thorne is the father, he devises a plan to get his old friend out of town. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Two men claiming to be IRA sympathisers approach Thomas in the Garrison and offer to buy the missing crate of guns they have heard he has in his possession. The gang leader also goes to Cheltenham races with Grace, where he aims to persuade kingpin Billy Kimber to make the Peaky Blinders part of his operation, while Ada secretly marries communist agitator Freddie Thorne. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Brecon Beacons with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the unique scenery of the Brecon Beacons over the course of a year, braving the elements in every season to detail the ever-changing landscape of the valleys. He begins in the winter, enduring a raging blizzard on the high peaks while the lowlands are bathed in sun. On the snowy slopes, foxes search for food while the great grey shrike - a ruthless hunter from Scandinavia - seeks other animals to eat. Plus, Iolo tracks red deer to the secluded gullies in one of the Beacons' wildest locations(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian explores Northern Ireland's role in the Second World War, revealing the story of a pilot who became the most successful U-boat hunter of all time. The programme also examines Harland & Wolff's contributions to the development of the famous Churchill tank, and takes an underwater look at the wreck of the U-155. In Donegal, a major excavation reveals the 70-year-old Spitfire flown by American pilot Bud Wolfe, who was forced to bail out just minutes after taking off(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez heads to Mexico, where, just before the outbreak of the Second World War, American husband-and-wife explorer team Matthew and Marion Stirling were lured into the jungle by the legend of a colossal stone head. They found the head - and a lot more than they bargained for - because it turned out to be the first clue in a trail that led to the discovery of a lost civilisation, now known as the Olmecs(n)

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver continues his attempt to shed new light on the Norse warriors by examining their sophisticated trading skills, and visits locations in Russia, Turkey and Ireland to explain how they established a business empire. He also explores the vast array of goods that were bought and sold by Viking merchants and settlers, including Chinese silks and Pictish slaves(n)

programme.title: Stories from the Dark Earth: Meet the Ancestors Revisited
programme.desc: Archaeologist Julian Richards revisits some of his most important digs to discover how science, conservation and new finds have changed people's understanding of ancient history. His journey begins at the excavation of two burials from Roman Britain - a wealthy man from Winchester, Hampshire, and the lavishly appointed grave of a woman from the heart of London(n)

programme.title: Arthur

Jamie has to start at a new school mid-term, but his amazing footballing talents should have no problem fitting in with the cool kids. However, new friend Jack is disgusted when she witnesses him being cruel to the geeky Boggie. Louis Dunn stars(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Jamie is determined to track down his father, and comes up with a clever plan after new friend Boggie displays a talent for lateral thinking(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson

Jack and Boggie try to help Jamie deal with Hansard - with Jack persuading Jamie that an apology would help and Boggie using his guile to cement Jamie's second chance. When the truth comes out about why his father really left, Jamie lashes out, threatening to ruin everything all over again. However, a selfless act on the pitch shows Hansard what Jamie's really made of, and out of it comes a prize beyond Jamie's wildest dreams(n)

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: Comedian Owen Money visits Merthyr Borough Ex-Servicemen's Club, the place where he first performed in public aged 16. The town has produced many champion boxers through the years and Owen discovers the gym above the club continuing that tradition. Currently, it's where Welsh Commonwealth Games boxer Lynsey Holdaway trains. As Owen sets about re-staging his first gig at the club with familiar faces in the crowd, there is a shocking turn of events(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked by the hostility of the other mums at Ollie's new school, and shares her concerns with Mick. Louise hatches a plan to help Bex take her mind off university. Kathy learns Bobby is staying with Max and pleads with her grandson to reconsider. Phil and Sharon realise Mel has been taking money from the E20 account and cut her off - leaving her unable to pay for Hunter's funeral(n)

programme.title: Eddie Butler's Rugby World Cup
programme.desc: The former Wales forward previews the tournament in Japan and looks back at memorable moments from past tournaments. programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: TV and radio presenter Rickie Haywood-Williams, legendary Motown singer Martha Reeves, Olympic gold medal-winning long jumper Greg Rutherford, actress Elizabeth Bourgine and heavyweight boxer Dillian Whyte take part, as the competition hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace continues. The first challenge for the five hopefuls is an Invention Test - the infamous test of imagination and creativity. The contestants are all given a mincer and a burger press and are tasked with making just one dish - a burger and fries - of their own design. Ingredients to choose from include tuna, mackerel, beef chuck, pork belly, lamb shoulder and turkey breast(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby wakes up in a park and heads back to the Square, where his behaviour concerns Callum - but when he tries to help the troubled teen runs off and bumps into Rainie. Kathy and Ian blame each other for Bobby struggling to settle back into Walford, and Mel speaks to Jay about scaling back Hunter's funeral - but she is stunned with what he tells her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric fears for his health, leading him to withdraw just as his granddaughter goes into premature labour. Cameron steps in to take charge, and forces Ric to face up to his fears. Essie's false claim to be Isla's adoptive mother stands to be exposed when the girl requires emergency surgery, and Chloe throws herself back into work in the hope of moving on from Evan - but is gripped by a paranoid belief that someone is spying on her(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Shaun is interrogated by the CIA in a covert safe house, and while Rachel sees CCTV footage of him going in, she then finds she is unable to locate him inside. Her investigation leads her to make shocking discoveries about the degree to which CCTV footage might be manipulated. Finally released by his American captors, Shaun goes on the run from the police - but his quest to clear his name soon takes a devastating turn(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Detective Hoggy is on the hunt for a heroin addict and prolific thief who is on the run for multiple offences. When they finally catch him, he makes it clear that he has nothing left to lose, and is ready to give the team a tough time when they take him in for questioning. Elsewhere, a 27-year-old addict has been arrested 69 times as she continues offending to fund her habit(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at the Elizabeth Garrett Anderson school in North London(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas: HIV and Me
programme.desc: Hard-hitting documentary following Gareth Thomas as he opens up about living with HIV, and sets about tackling the stigmas, myths and misunderstandings surrounding the HIV virus. The programme follows Gareth as he prepares to take on the biggest physical challenge of his career - running the world's toughest Iron Man(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at life in the In The Style offices in Greater Manchester and the new world of ultra-fast fashion. The series follows the work of 32-year-old business owner Adam Frisby and his team during the summer season, as the company competes with rival fast fashion brands to sign up the latest talent, come up with the hottest styles and supply them faster and better than any other brand. In the first episode, social media influencer Lorna Luxe launches her new summer range and has big plans for a glamorous shoot(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Landing at Glenuig, Paul Murton makes his way inland to Loch Moidart and MacDonald country, walking the old coffin road, which was used for centuries by funeral parties, taking their dead to a sacred isle. On the track Paul meets a trials bike enthusiast and learns the secrets of a 'dabbing coat' before landing off the Green Isle, where he rings a 1,100-year-old bell for the dead. Taking a boat north, Paul sails up Loch Shiel to Glenfinnan where Bonnie Prince Charlie officially began his rising to reclaim the throne for the Stuart Dynasty. From a forgotten monument that commemorates this event, Paul heads back to the coast and Loch Ailort, where he learns how to knife fight before taking a trip across the loch to a deserted community on the roadless Ardnish peninsula(n)

Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen(n)

programme.desc: Gabriel's intervening when Dougie spirals downwards has serious consequences, while Lou resorts to dirty tactics against Lenny and AJ tries to connect with a misbehaving Bex(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Shaun is interrogated by the CIA in a covert safe house, and while Rachel sees CCTV footage of him going in, she then finds she is unable to locate him inside. Her investigation leads her to make shocking discoveries about the degree to which CCTV footage might be manipulated. Finally released by his American captors, Shaun goes on the run from the police - but his quest to clear his name soon takes a devastating turn(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric fears for his health, leading him to withdraw just as his granddaughter goes into premature labour. Cameron steps in to take charge, and forces Ric to face up to his fears. Essie's false claim to be Isla's adoptive mother stands to be exposed when the girl requires emergency surgery, and Chloe throws herself back into work in the hope of moving on from Evan - but is gripped by a paranoid belief that someone is spying on her(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Detective Hoggy is on the hunt for a heroin addict and prolific thief who is on the run for multiple offences. When they finally catch him, he makes it clear that he has nothing left to lose, and is ready to give the team a tough time when they take him in for questioning. Elsewhere, a 27-year-old addict has been arrested 69 times as she continues offending to fund her habit(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: The Papers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Cameras follow three different newspapers, The Herald, The National and Evening Times, as they cover one of the biggest political stories of a generation, Brexit. As Brexit reaches fever-pitch, The Herald and National titles are launching new Sunday versions and covering a major political story, while budgets are cut and resources become stretched(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at life in the In The Style offices in Greater Manchester and the new world of ultra-fast fashion. The series follows the work of 32-year-old business owner Adam Frisby and his team during the summer season, as the company competes with rival fast fashion brands to sign up the latest talent, come up with the hottest styles and supply them faster and better than any other brand. In the first episode, social media influencer Lorna Luxe launches her new summer range and has big plans for a glamorous shoot(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: Jo Scott helps out with this year's cauliflower harvest, while reporter Ruth Sanderson meets a group of lawnmower racing enthusiasts(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked by the hostility of the other mums at Ollie's new school, and shares her concerns with Mick. Louise hatches a plan to help Bex take her mind off university. Kathy learns Bobby is staying with Max and pleads with her grandson to reconsider. Phil and Sharon realise Mel has been taking money from the E20 account and cut her off - leaving her unable to pay for Hunter's funeral(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: TV and radio presenter Rickie Haywood-Williams, legendary Motown singer Martha Reeves, Olympic gold medal-winning long jumper Greg Rutherford, actress Elizabeth Bourgine and heavyweight boxer Dillian Whyte take part, as the competition hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace continues. The first challenge for the five hopefuls is an Invention Test - the infamous test of imagination and creativity. The contestants are all given a mincer and a burger press and are tasked with making just one dish - a burger and fries - of their own design. Ingredients to choose from include tuna, mackerel, beef chuck, pork belly, lamb shoulder and turkey breast(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Revealing documentary following a Belfast strippogram agency and its performers - including the most popular act, Balaclava Woman(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: It's the most important day of the year for Kerry - the annual steam fair. This year it falls on her birthday and Kurtan has organised her a surprise present. However, the gift involves some walking, and so the cousins embark on a cross-country trek. This affords them the time to share some local village myths with the crew, such as the legend of the 'Fox Twins'(n)

programme.title: Murder in Successville
programme.desc: Sleet and sidekick Emma Bunton go to North Successville, where the detective investigates the murder of a fellow cop and takes a liking to the area's chief of police. Three likely suspects soon emerge - the owner of a diner, a local fisherman and a disgraced former police officer. An approaching storm forces Sleet and Emma to seek shelter in a forest - which leads to a showdown with the killer(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Interview
programme.desc: It has been three years since David Cameron stepped down as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, following the country's momentous vote to leave the European Union. Now, in his first in-depth television interview about his time in office, David Cameron talks to Tom Bradby about the highs and lows of his premiership, along with his thoughts on the 2016 referendum and its consequences for the UK(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Billy asks questions about Paul's past, and Ali fails to hide his struggles from Toyah. Elsewhere, Aggie defies Ed and plans a surprise party behind his back(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: Detective Superintendent Steve Fulcher takes chief suspect Christopher Halliwell to a remote location in the Wiltshire countryside to question him about the whereabouts of missing Swindon girl, Sian O'Callaghan. When Fulcher manages to gain Halliwell's trust, events take a turn that will change the lives of both men for ever, as Halliwell confesses to killing Sian along with a prostitute he murdered and buried in a field many years earlier. Drama, starring Martin Freeman, Joe Absolom and Siobhan Finneran(n)

programme.title: Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster meets Mark Riebe, who was convicted for one murder but has confessed multiple times to the abduction and killing of 13 women along Florida's Gulf Coast. Although Riebe has since withdrawn his confessions, police believe he is one of the worst serial killers in Florida's history. Piers uses Riebe's video and audio confessions, news archive and testimony to explore what sort of man would commit murder and then taunt the victims' families for nearly 20 years(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Traumatised from the attack, Roz makes her way home and finds a furious Joanne waiting for her. With no one else to confide in, Roz decides to tell Scott about Wayne, who promises to retrieve the footage. Meanwhile, Lisa finds Joe in a local bar and watches as he drinks and flirts with the barmaid. When she awakes the next morning and realises he has not come home, she fears the worst. With her daughter's return, Kate struggles to keep her web of lies intact. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan, Steven Cree and Gerald Kyd(n)

When Fulcher manages to gain Halliwell's trust, events take a turn that will change the lives of both men for ever, as Halliwell confesses to killing Sian along with a prostitute he murdered and buried in a field many years earlier. programme.title: Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster meets Mark Riebe, who was convicted for one murder but has confessed multiple times to the abduction and killing of 13 women along Florida's Gulf Coast. Although Riebe has since withdrawn his confessions, police believe he is one of the worst serial killers in Florida's history. Piers uses Riebe's video and audio confessions, news archive and testimony to explore what sort of man would commit murder and then taunt the victims' families for nearly 20 years(n)

With no one else to confide in, Roz decides to tell Scott about Wayne, who promises to retrieve the footage. Meanwhile, Lisa finds Joe in a local bar and watches as he drinks and flirts with the barmaid. When she awakes the next morning and realises he has not come home, she fears the worst. programme.desc: Two couples disappear in the tiny village of Monks Barton amid rumours of witchcraft and haunted woods. As Barnaby and Jones investigate, psychic Cyrus LeVanu clashes with cleric Wallace Stone over their different methods of 'healing' the woods, while other locals seem to be caught up in a stolen antiques racket. When a body is unearthed and the death toll rises, the detectives race to catch the killer by finding a connection between the missing couples and the thefts. Anton Lesser guest stars, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The village is plagued by a spate of burglaries, and the finger of suspicion points to a most unlikely perpetrator, revealing a heartbreaking tale of domestic abuse and family tragedy. Elsewhere, Vernon resolves to dig out his books on hypnotism and put an end to Gina's arachnophobia, but his early experiments on David leave his patient feeling rather peculiar, while Dennis and Jenny discover a mutual love of jazz(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica Fletcher tackles a mystery involving a diamond, a dog and an old grudge while on holiday at a friend's lodge in the Grand Teton mountains. A surly visitor who threatened to reveal some scandalous information about her friend is found dead. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Pauline finds out about Martin's imminent departure to London and confronts him over her uncertain future. Aunt Joan tries to solve her farm's money problems by opening a B&B - and ends up having to call the doctor for her first guest, local farmer Ted. Elsewhere, Bert organises a baby shower for Louisa. David Ross (The Green Green Grass) guest stars, with Martin Clunes, Stephanie Cole and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor spends his last day in Portwenn seeing an influx of patients as he prepares to close down the surgery and move to London. As he bids farewell to old friends and sets off to leave the village, he sees Louisa's teaching assistant Tasha fall from the Harbour Day stage and stops to help. But the bigger hold-up comes when pregnant Louisa is involved in a car accident. Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A gangland killing takes a surprising twist when it turns out the victim has been sexually mutilated. Andy punishes Janet by keeping her out of the heart of the investigation, and when she rebels, the ensuing row becomes personal. Rachel feels ambushed by Sean, who announces his mother wants to meet her - especially as she has spent years trying not to think about her own mum. Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Sean Maguire and Nicholas Gleaves star(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers Nick has been brutally assaulted - and she is top of the list of suspects. But even worse, she has no recollection of the previous night after a drunken hen party, and struggles to convince herself she did not do it. Janet thinks she has made her peace with Andy, until he gives her some misinformation that results in Gill missing an appointment with the coroner - so the DCI, tired of their games, announces one of them will have to go. A man's accidental death turns into a murder investigation when his daughters reveal their mother often talked of killing him. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is annoyed that Mike did not tell her he was going to the school to talk about Mark. Raquel feels Gordon does not compare with Colin and begins to go off him(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica Fletcher tackles a mystery involving a diamond, a dog and an old grudge while on holiday at a friend's lodge in the Grand Teton mountains. A surly visitor who threatened to reveal some scandalous information about her friend is found dead. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and Stan volunteer Olive as supervisor for the depot nursery. However, when she breaks her glasses and has to go home for a spare pair the clueless duo are left holding the fort. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Anna Karen(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan believes living at home is cramping his style and resolves to find his own place. Vintage comedy with Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby manages to frighten Alan by kidding him the house is haunted by a spectral 'grey lady' - but ends up getting a taste of his own medicine. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop's hoard of cash causes trouble for the family when Marcus Cope resolves to stop Charley buying the hop garden at any cost. Montgomery is unhappy at naval college and the Brigadier's solution provides a challenge for Pop(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The County Sheepdog Trials take place at Ashfordly Hall, and it soon becomes clear that even such a prestigious event can bring out the worst in some people. Meanwhile, Vernon tries to cash in by organising the toilet facilities for the event - but his money-making dreams soon go down the pan(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel plans to disrupt the Boxing Day hunt at Robblefield, and after spending the day drinking, Frank heads to the hunt to confront Neil(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim attempts to contact Frank, Zoe urges him to speak to her, and then finds Frank destroying Kim's possessions. Carol is touched when Lorraine asks to come home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: After Mavis tells him he is getting fat, Derek takes up jogging. Elaine alerts Curly to the high shoplifting rate at the store, and he offends the staff by searching their bags(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf puts an offer in on the bungalow, Jack wants to enter a talent contest at the Queens, and Derek is told to rest for a week(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The County Sheepdog Trials take place at Ashfordly Hall, and it soon becomes clear that even such a prestigious event can bring out the worst in some people. Meanwhile, Vernon tries to cash in by organising the toilet facilities for the event - but his money-making dreams soon go down the pan(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is called in to investigate the case of a deputy sheriff accused of murdering a businessman against whom he held a grudge - and soon finds there is more to it than meets the eye. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frustrated that a number of cases have been knocked back by the Crown Prosecution Service, Banks and the team are called on to investigate the murder of teenager Ellie Clayton, daughter of a high-profile internet entrepreneur. The victim's background, together with the suggestion she may have been raped, means the incident is soon the subject of much media speculation, and strong evidence implicates a theatre director who ran a drama workshop the girl attended on the night she died. The detective believes he has found his man, but Morton fears her colleague isn't seeing the bigger picture(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The collapse of Owen Pierce's trial has left the case wide open and there's still a killer on the loose, but Banks remains determined to build a watertight legal case against his prime suspect. As the investigation continues, Morton and Jackman uncover promising new leads focusing on Ellie's ex-boyfriend Tyler, but just as they seem to be getting close to the truth, another schoolgirl is murdered, putting Pierce in the spotlight once again(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. Fortunately, a vacationing Poirot - who shares with most great detectives an inability to go on holiday without stumbling over a body or two - is on hand to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell(n)
